comment_text.sn40.1:1.1: This chapter is similar to SN 21.

root_text.sn40.1:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahāmoggallāno sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn40.1:1.2: Tatra kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn40.1:1.4: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.sn40.1:2.1: “Idha mayhaṁ, āvuso, rahogatassa paṭisallīnassa evaṁ cetaso parivitakko udapādi:
root_text.sn40.1:2.5: ‘idha bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.sn40.1:2.7: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharāmi.
root_text.sn40.1:2.8: Tassa mayhaṁ, āvuso, iminā vihārena viharato kāmasahagatā saññāmanasikārā samudācaranti.
root_text.sn40.1:3.1: Atha kho maṁ, āvuso, bhagavā iddhiyā upasaṅkamitvā etadavoca:
root_text.sn40.1:3.3: Mā, brāhmaṇa, paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ pamādo, paṭhame jhāne cittaṁ saṇṭhapehi, paṭhame jhāne cittaṁ ekodiṁ karohi, paṭhame jhāne cittaṁ samādahā’ti.
root_text.sn40.1:3.4: So khvāhaṁ, āvuso, aparena samayena vivicceva kāmehi vivicca akusalehi dhammehi savitakkaṁ savicāraṁ vivekajaṁ pītisukhaṁ paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja vihāsiṁ.
root_text.sn40.1:3.5: Yañhi taṁ, āvuso, sammā vadamāno vadeyya:
root_text.sn40.1:3.6: ‘satthārānuggahito sāvako mahābhiññataṁ patto’ti, mamaṁ taṁ sammā vadamāno vadeyya:

translation_text.sn40.1:0.3: A Question About the First Absorption
translation_text.sn40.1:1.1: At one time Venerable Mahāmoggallāna was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn40.1:1.2: There Venerable Mahāmoggallāna addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn40.1:2.1: “Just now, reverends, as I was in private retreat this thought came to mind:
translation_text.sn40.1:2.2: ‘They speak of this thing called the “first absorption”.
translation_text.sn40.1:2.5: ‘It’s when a mendicant, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters and remains in the first absorption, which has the rapture and bliss born of seclusion, while placing the mind and keeping it connected.
translation_text.sn40.1:2.6: This is called the first absorption.’
translation_text.sn40.1:2.7: And so … I was entering and remaining in the first absorption.
translation_text.sn40.1:2.8: While I was in that meditation, perception and focus accompanied by sensual pleasures beset me.
translation_text.sn40.1:3.1: Then the Buddha came up to me with his psychic power and said,
translation_text.sn40.1:3.3: Don’t neglect the first absorption, brahmin! Settle your mind in the first absorption; unify your mind and immerse it in the first absorption.’
translation_text.sn40.1:3.4: And so, after some time … I entered and remained in the first absorption.
translation_text.sn40.1:3.5: So if anyone should be rightly called
translation_text.sn40.1:3.6: a disciple who attained to great direct knowledge with help from the Teacher, it’s me.”
